vulnerabilities.description: Homepage HTML comment exposes test account credentials (test:test), enabling unauthorized access.

Flask signed session cookie (session=...) is issued without the Secure attribute and the app is served over HTTP, increasing risk of session theft. Cookie contains only user_id, suggesting potential session forgery if SECRET_KEY is weak.
Receipt endpoint returns 200 for arbitrary IDs (e.g., 999999), indicating lack of strict existence/authorization checks and possible cross-user data exposure if valid IDs are guessed.
Server header reveals Apache version and OS (Apache/2.4.66 (Debian)).
Forms (including logout) lack visible CSRF tokens, suggesting susceptibility to CSRF for state-changing actions.
Password form exposes internal user_id in a hidden field. Although tampering appeared overridden by the server in testing, exposing identifiers increases the risk of logic/authorization flaws.
